message: So my neighbor knocked on the door during last week's outage, flashlight in hand, asking if we had any battery-powered lights to spare. And I—ME, the person who usually shows up to potlucks empty-handed—actually had a couple of lanterns sitting right there in the closet. I felt like I'd finally leveled up in the adulting game. 😄 We set one on her kitchen counter, plugged a phone into my power bank, and suddenly we both felt a lot calmer. Turns out "just having a plan" is way less stressful than scrambling in the dark. I grabbed our family's blackout checklist recently and it honestly made me feel so much more grounded about the whole thing. What's your go-to move when the lights go out?
